A.structural_reexpression_note: Basin A imports the source-backed C1/T6 support nodes and support digest, then declares new C3-specific edge/port ids for the competing-basin fixture. The new edges and ports are design extensions, not prior runtime evidence.

B.source_backing_requirement: Iteration 9-B must emit source-backed B support/probe rows before B can be used as compatibility evidence. This design row alone is not evidence for B-basin identity.

metrics.metric_semantics: Count support-affecting fields not present in the declared support_area_digest_input or source support rows. The metric passes only when no hidden support fields are consumed.

competition_semantics.claim_boundary: A positive result would support compatibility of an ID5 basin near another basin; it would not prove semantic choice, agency, identity acceptance, RC identity collapse, or biological identity.
competition_semantics.definition: Competing basins are structurally coupled alternatives on the same coherence/flux field. They share or border a local neighborhood U such that coherence, flux, support evidence, or basin legibility can be captured by, leaked into, or disturbed by either basin.
competition_semantics.iteration_9b_question: Does Basin A remain distinct, coherent, and replayable as A when Basin B is source-backed and present in the shared-U compatibility fixture?

composite_topology.primitive_extension_policy: The frozen Iteration 2 C3 manifest declares a minimal T2/T3 compatibility sketch. Iteration 9 extends C3 after the Iteration 8 C1/T6 closeout by composing the closed ID5 chain with T7 compatibility. The frozen manifest is not rewritten; 9-B/9-C use this fixture as the authoritative C3/T7 extension contract.

manifest_extension_policy.rationale: Changing n07_fixture_manifest_v1 after Iteration 2 would invalidate pinned prior artifacts. Iteration 9 therefore declares T7 as a post-closeout fixture extension and records that downstream C3/T7 iterations validate against this fixture.

source_control_replay_requirements.iteration_9b: emit probe/control rows from the frozen fixture
source_control_replay_requirements.iteration_9c: replay 9-B source probe/control rows from artifacts only

t7_becoming_method_guidance.note: T7 compatibility can strengthen an artifact-only ID candidate, but does not by itself authorize identity acceptance, RC identity collapse, agency, or semantic choice.

activity_history.orientation: N07 Iteration 8 C1/T6 artifact-only replay closeout
N07 Iteration 8 C1/T6 artifact-only replay closeout

gate_vector_schema.lineage_current_condition: lineage_current must pass when topology_mutation_occurs is true; otherwise lineage_current is not_applicable

t4_deferral.rationale: T4 is the recurrence/no-mutation baseline and must be run before interpreting topology-free recurrence as stronger than ID4.

available_native_surfaces.claim_boundary: surface row is not identity carrier by itself
lineage evidence is not RC identity acceptance
reabsorption is runtime hygiene, not identity
budget conservation is necessary but insufficient
persistence window is evidence, not unrestricted identity
no runtime identity-acceptance event in Iteration 1
available_native_surfaces.n07_use: only as explicit native contract if present; otherwise blocked

external_sources_by_title.n07_use: classify what appeared before probing; promote only reusable, generative, coherence-preserving classes
external_sources_by_title.title: Classification of Becoming

external_sources_by_title.n07_use: treat probes as bounded questions and record the crossed boundary rung without proof inflation
external_sources_by_title.title: Interrogation of Becoming

external_sources_by_title.n07_use: separate probe-exposed capacity from native regime expression and record support-dependence
external_sources_by_title.title: Naturalization of Becoming

external_sources_by_title.n07_use: record orient/observe/classify/probe/withdraw/naturalize/integrate activity history and cultivate functions rather than proxies
external_sources_by_title.title: Cultivation of Becoming

id_ladder_schema.activity_history_digest_method: sha256 of canonical JSON over orient/observe/classify/probe/withdraw/naturalize/integrate events; null if no activity history exists
id_ladder_schema.carrier_rule: ID1-ID6 candidate rows require candidate_identity_carrier_type == coherence_basin
id_ladder_schema.experiment_local_observables_used_format: list of declared experiment-local observable ids consumed by the row
id_ladder_schema.native_observables_used_format: list of runtime-visible LGRC/native observable ids consumed by the row
id_ladder_schema.source_artifacts_format: list of objects with name, path, exists, sha256, status, claim_ceiling, primary_blocker when available
id_ladder_schema.source_reports_format: list of objects with name, path, exists, sha256
id_ladder_schema.visual_is_evidence_source_format: boolean; must be false for Iteration 1 and for any visual-only reference without source artifact backing
id_ladder_schema.visual_reference_format: optional relative path or null; visual references are illustrative unless visual_is_evidence_source is explicitly true

identity_carrier_taxonomy.rule: non-coherence-basin carriers cannot derive an ID ceiling above ID0

theory_gates.n07_observable: directed-flux attraction domain around sink/basin chart
gradient, Hessian/well proxy, net flux, basin mass, id, parent, depth
nine-port chart, sink relation, spark/refinement boundary
lineage-current support, no stale node id replay
declared stable well / retention / Hessian proxy
support overlap, lineage-current overlap, proper-time persistence
re-entry maintains or strengthens consumed basin evidence
agency remains blocked; identity is prerequisite only
